last_completed_item: D-224 — Versioned save migrations with protected rollback
current_item: D-225 — Debug tools, cheats, and developer-command policy
next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting how debug tools cheats and developer commands are handled across developer internal external-test release-candidate and public Windows builds: strip almost all debug capabilities outside the Unity editor; ship a broadly accessible console and cheat system in every build; or use a capability-gated diagnostic command framework where development and approved test artifacts expose auditable commands for room warps loadouts enemy spawning save inspection checkpoint simulation performance overlays and fault injection, while public builds exclude progression-altering commands and retain only safe support diagnostics through explicit hidden or support-approved activation. Preserve immutable promoted artifacts, privacy-safe local logs, versioned save migration, achievements and records integrity, offline play, future speedrunning, automated testing, and one-click support bundles. Include accidental exposure, security through obscurity limits, tester convenience, reproduction speed, build-configuration drift, save contamination, achievement invalidation, deterministic test setup, fault injection, command auditing, and whether legitimate accessibility or practice features should be implemented separately from cheats. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. D-221 through D-224 are checkpointed as queued but not formally batch-persisted; continue through D-230 unless the user changes the cadence or requests a handoff.
resume_instruction: Read assembly/context/NEW_CHAT_RESUME.md first and follow it exactly.
blockers: Recovered decisions D-001 through D-039 require re-verification before final requirements
The product repository uses a bootstrap kit installed before the latest handoff framework additions; current resume files were added directly for this test

The authoritative Intake Interviewer prompt path assembly/prompts/00-intake-interviewer.md is currently missing from this branch and should be restored from the framework before final intake generation
The D-101 through D-220 decisions are persisted as authoritative batch-extension artifacts and should be consolidated into the primary live log and session snapshot during a later maintenance pass

persistence.batching_rule: Checkpoint queued state after several questions for flow; formally persist D-221 through D-230 at D-230 unless an earlier handoff is requested

constraints: Ordinary branches run compilation fast deterministic edit-mode tests content validation generated-registry checks and tests targeted to affected systems
Before integration run relevant play-mode smoke tests and a Windows player smoke build covering launch menu input room load combat save resume and clean exit

Scheduled or milestone validation runs full regression representative performance and memory scenes save interruption recovery and migration checks clean-machine installation offline launch and accessibility settings persistence
Required integration checks retain identifiable artifacts and actionable diagnostics
Flaky tests are quarantined and repaired rather than repeatedly rerun until green
Android networking and complete co-op pipelines remain deferred until those workstreams begin

Each candidate artifact records unique build and content versions source commit Unity version dependency and generated-content fingerprints test reports symbols checksums and save-schema compatibility
The exact same artifact is promoted through developer internal external release-candidate and public channels
Any code content configuration or dependency change creates a new artifact rather than silently altering a tested build
Retain the current public or formal build the previous known-good build relevant migration tools and a save-safe rollback procedure
Formal external Stage 1 tests and Stage 2 milestones use identifiable immutable artifacts even if earlier prototypes use a lighter process

Local bounded logs record build content and save-schema versions session and major mission outcomes deaths restarts checkpoints banking loadout changes performance failures exceptions asset failures and save journal recovery results
Relevant input-device and settings state may be included without personal identifiers
Logs remain local by default and testers explicitly choose when to export or submit them
Formal test builds explain what is recorded and exclude or redact free-text names account data filenames and unrelated system information
Logs rotate automatically and a one-click support bundle combines diagnostics configuration build identity and optional tester notes
Remote telemetry remains a future opt-in consideration only if project scale justifies its privacy security hosting and retention costs
Every save records game content and schema versions migration history and stable content identifiers
Newer builds create immutable backups then validate and apply ordered idempotent tested migrations before atomically committing the result
Failed or interrupted migrations restore safely and older builds refuse to modify newer-schema saves
Rollback uses cloned saves or compatible backups rather than destructive downgrade
Removed content uses stable-ID tombstones replacements refunds or explicit compensation rules
Development channels may permit announced resets but archive or export old profiles first while public compatibility promises remain explicit and tested
